description_long: This object is a metal guard rail commonly used in urban environments to control pedestrian or vehicle movement. It features a rectangular frame with evenly spaced vertical bars, all constructed from cylindrical metal tubing. The guard rail stands on two angled legs that provide stability and prevent tipping, with each leg extending outward from the base. The surface appears to be coated in a dark, likely powder-coated or painted finish, offering weather resistance and durability. The ends of the rail are blunt and capped, with no visible sharp edges or additional safety features. There are no reflective markings or decorative elements, giving it a utilitarian and robust appearance suitable for sidewalks, parks, or event crowd control.
description: A sturdy, dark metal guard rail with vertical bars and angled support legs, designed for urban crowd or traffic control. Its simple, utilitarian design features a smooth, weather-resistant finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the guard rail with evenly spaced vertical bars and both angled support legs visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile of the rail, with the cylindrical main rail and the outward-angled support legs clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the vertical bars and support legs from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, showing the slim profile and the outward-angled legs.
